title: pastinaca mazurevskii
intro: Pastinaca mazurevskii, also known as Russian hog fennel, is a type of perennial plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The plant's roots and seeds are rich in volatile oils, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and indigestion. Herbal preparations of Pastinaca mazurevskii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote digestion, relieve congestion, and support overall well-being.
